summary: Large item-system resplit of src/melee/it/it_2725.c into smaller item translation units. The PR added split metadata, symbols metadata, new headers/sources for ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako, updated configure.py so all new units except itmaplib are Matching, and adjusted includes/type names across item, fighter, and ground users.

key_files.role: Original large item TU reduced from 0x80272560-0x8027CF00 to 0x80272560-0x802753DC and retained as a smaller unclear-purpose item unit.
New split for item hitbox setup, scaling, state toggles, and hitbox-derived collision queries.
New split for item map collision behavior; configured NonMatching while other new TUs were Matching.
evidence_refs: pr_body: filename found in __assert("itmaplib.c", ...); all matching and linked except itmaplib

key_files.role: New split for item material/render-state manipulation involving HSD_MObj, HSD_TExp, render mode, and GXColor state.
evidence_refs: pr_body: `itmaterial` description and struct it_MObjInfo it_803F1F90 filename evidence

key_files.role: New split for item effect helpers, effect archive setup, and randomized sync effects.
New split for item animation-command handlers using CommandInfo* and animation-list side effects.
New split aggregating Pokémon, character-specific, monster, and stage-item tables plus shared helpers.
evidence_refs: pr_body: `it_279C` description; Codex suggested `itspecial` with low confidence

key_files.role: New split for Zako/enemy-style items and grzakogenerator-related item code.
evidence_refs: pr_body: filename inferred from "can t init zako pos" string
diff: grzakogenerator include changed from it_2725.h to itzako.h

key_files.role: Split ownership metadata updated to carve it_2725.c into seven additional item TUs with text/data/sdata/sdata2 ranges.
evidence_refs: diff: added melee/it/ithitbox.c, itmaplib.c, itmaterial.c, iteffect.c, itanimlist.c, it_279C.c, itzako.c split ranges

key_files.role: Symbol metadata adjusted for new local/global ownership, local labels, sizes, and data-section placement around the resplit.
evidence_refs: diff: global-to-local scope changes for strings/floats and local label additions such as ...data.0

key_files.role: Build matching configuration updated for the new item TUs.
evidence_refs: diff: it_2725, ithitbox, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, itzako set Matching; itmaplib set NonMatching

what_changed: PR changed 152 files with 9610 additions and 9954 deletions according to metadata.
The old broad it_2725.c split was narrowed and new item split entries were added for ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako.
New headers were added for the new split units, including it_279C.h, itmaplib.h, itzako.h, itanimlist.h, ithitbox.h, itmaterial.h, and iteffect.h.
configure.py moved it_2725.c and most new split units to Matching while keeping itmaplib.c NonMatching.
Symbols metadata around the old it_2725 range was revised, including multiple global-to-local scope changes for data, rodata, sdata, sdata2, and sbss objects.
Callers in fighter and ground code were updated to include more specific new item headers such as it_279C.h, ithitbox.h, and itzako.h instead of or in addition to it_2725.h.
Type names were made more semantic in visible excerpts, e.g. it_27CF_DatAttrs to itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs, it_279D_ItemVars to itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars, and it_2F28_DatAttrs to itUnk2_DatAttrs.

smart_moves.text: The author explicitly documented weak names and low-confidence AI suggestions instead of silently promoting them: it_2725 purpose remained unclear and `itspecial` for it_279C was noted as low confidence.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Purpose still unclear; Codex suggested calling it `itcommon`
pr_body: Codex suggested `itspecial`, but with low confidence

smart_moves.text: The resplit was backed by several kinds of TU-boundary evidence: repeated floats/strings, assert filenames, a named struct object, and a string associated with zako initialization.
evidence_refs: pr_body: split based on repeated floats/strings

smart_moves.text: The PR kept itmaplib as NonMatching despite the broader resplit, matching the author's statement that it was the exception among the new linked/matching units.
evidence_refs: pr_body: All of these are matching and linked except `itmaplib`

smart_moves.text: More specific headers replaced broad it_2725.h dependencies where excerpts show callsites clearly moved to the newly split API surface.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCo_HammerWait.c include changed to it/it_279C.h
diff: fighter.c include changed to it/it_279C.h
diff: grzakogenerator.c include changed to it/itzako.h
diff: grcastle.c and grmaterial.c add it/ithitbox.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large item TUs, TU split boundaries can be informed by clusters of repeated floats/strings, assert filenames, object names, and distinctive string literals, but low-confidence semantic filenames should stay conservative.
evidence_refs: pr_body: repeated floats/strings used for split iteration
pr_body: Filename inferred from "can t init zako pos" string

decomp_lessons.lesson: A resplit can produce apparent broken matches in the source unit while producing corresponding new matches in the destination units; review the movement by unit, not just the old unit's regressions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: 132 broken matches in main/melee/it/it_2725
pr_body: resplit it_2725 into a bunch of TUs

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a split exposes more specific ownership, update includes and prototypes to depend on the narrower owning header rather than leaving broad legacy includes.
evidence_refs: diff: it_279C.h, itmaplib.h, itzako.h, itanimlist.h, ithitbox.h added
diff: ftCo_HammerWait.c and fighter.c include it_279C.h

naming_conventions.text: Prefer functional split names when direct evidence exists: ithitbox for hitbox-related helpers, itmaplib for assert-backed map collision helpers, itmaterial for material/render helpers, iteffect for effect helpers, itanimlist for assert-backed animation-list handlers, and itzako for zako/enemy-style items.
evidence_refs: pr_body descriptions for ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, itzako

naming_conventions.text: Keep address-style or unclear names when semantic meaning is not proven: it_2725 remained unclear, and it_279C was not renamed to low-confidence `itspecial`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: `it_279C`: Codex suggested `itspecial`, but with low confidence

naming_conventions.text: Visible type renames moved some opaque numbered item types toward more specific item roles, such as itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs and itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The PR used split and symbol metadata changes rather than only C edits: splits.txt received new text/data ranges and symbols.txt adjusted local/global ownership and object sizes around the old it_2725 range.
evidence_refs: diff: config/GALE01/symbols.txt scope and size edits

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The author used data-section clues for split discovery but did not claim semantic certainty for all names; AI-suggested names were documented as suggestions or low confidence.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Claude and Codex iteratively split based on repeated floats/strings
pr_body: Codex suggested itspecial with low confidence

notes: The loaded diff is truncated and changed_files.jsonl is truncated, so not all 152 changed paths can be individually inspected from loaded evidence.
PR body explicitly says AI tools proposed the split and some names; those suggestions are evidence of the author's process, not proof of original developer intent.
No inline review comments were available; review feedback consists of one approval and the automated report.
The bot report provides progress metrics, but this intake did not run builds, objdiff, or validation.
Path roles for new TUs are grounded in the PR author's descriptions and visible split metadata, not independently revalidated against current source.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use multiple TU-boundary signals for item resplits
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2546, the item resplit used repeated floats/strings, assert filenames, object names, and distinctive strings as split-boundary evidence. The same PR also kept low-confidence semantic names conservative, leaving it_2725 and it_279C address-style rather than adopting suggested names without stronger support.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret resplit match reports by source and destination units
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2546's automated report showed many broken matches in the original it_2725 unit alongside new matches in destination units. For resplit PRs, apparent regressions in the old unit may be expected movement if destination units gain corresponding matches.

source_update_candidates.title: Item split roles introduced by PR 2546
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2546 split the old it_2725.c range into it_2725, ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako. The author described ithitbox as item hitbox helpers, itmaplib as item map collision behavior, itmaterial as item material/render-state manipulation, iteffect as item effect helpers, itanimlist as item animation-command handlers, it_279C as Pokémon/character-specific/monster/stage-item tables plus helpers, and itzako as Zako/enemy-style items.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-role facts for future item decomp searches, but should be curated because some names were AI-assisted and not all semantic purposes are equally certain.

source_update_candidates.title: Resplit report interpretation
source_update_candidates.text: For resplit PRs, evaluate matched/broken report entries as movement between old and new units. Do not treat old-unit broken matches as regressions without checking destination-unit new matches and split metadata.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report with 157 new matches and 132 broken matches
pr_body: resplit it_2725 into multiple TUs
source_update_candidates.reason: Could extend verification guidance for resplit reviews; PR evidence supports the pattern but one PR may be insufficient for a global standard without curator review.

source_update_candidates.title: Conservative treatment of AI-suggested split names
source_update_candidates.text: When AI-assisted split exploration proposes semantic filenames, document low-confidence suggestions and keep address-style names when purpose is unclear rather than promoting speculative names.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Claude and Codex iteratively split it_2725
pr_body: it_2725 purpose unclear; Codex suggested itcommon
pr_body: it_279C Codex suggested itspecial with low confidence
source_update_candidates.reason: Consistent with conservative naming standards; curator may decide whether AI-assisted naming caution belongs in standards.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `itcommon` as the name or role for it_2725; the PR body says purpose was still unclear and only notes Codex suggested it.
Do not promote `itspecial` as the name or role for it_279C; the PR body explicitly says the suggestion was low confidence.
Do not claim itmaplib fully matched; the PR body and configure.py show it was the exception and remained NonMatching.
Do not infer reviewer rationale beyond the approving review text, which only says the PR was useful.
